steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content:
Both terms share a factor of $w^3$. Rewrite each term to make that common factor visible.
$$w^4=w^3\cdot w$$
$$620w^3=w^3\cdot 620$$

steps.title: Factor out the common part
steps.content:
Take $w^3$ out of both terms. The first term leaves $w$, and the second term leaves $620$.
$$w^4-620w^3=w^3(w-620)$$

steps.title: Check the expansion
steps.content: Expanding $w^3(w-620)$ gives $w^3\cdot w-w^3\cdot 620$, which simplifies to $w^4-620w^3$. That matches the original expression. The answer is D.
